# UC-22.13.43 · Post-Incident Lessons Learned Action Item Tracking

## Description

We track closure of lessons-learned tasks after CIP-008 incidents so corrective actions do not stall after the initial response.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes post_incident_action, post_incident_action`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Create ticket template for post-incident actions. (2) Weekly reminder digest. (3) Escalate overdue items to CISO office. (4) Link evidence in Splunk dashboards. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=post_incident_action`, `sourcetype=post_incident_action``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=post_incident_action`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create ticket template for post-incident actions. (2) Weekly reminder digest. (3) Escalate overdue items to CISO office. (4) Link evidence in Splunk dashboards.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=post_incident_action cip008=1 earliest=-365d
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval open=if(match(status,"(?i)closed"),0,1)
| where open=1 AND due_epoch < now()
| table action_id, incident_id, owner, due_date, status, description
```

Understanding this SPL

**Post-Incident Lessons Learned Action Item Tracking (CIP-008-6 R6)** — We track closure of lessons-learned tasks after CIP-008 incidents so corrective actions do not stall after the initial response.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=post_incident_action`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: post_incident_action. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=post_incident_action, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **open** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where open=1 AND due_epoch < now()` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: action_id, description, due_date, due_epoch, incident_id, open, owner, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue actions), Single value (count), Column chart (by owner)

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=post_incident_action cip008=1 earliest=-365d
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval open=if(match(status,"(?i)closed"),0,1)
| where open=1 AND due_epoch < now()
| table action_id, incident_id, owner, due_date, status, description
```

Table (overdue actions), Single value (count), Column chart (by owner)
